summary: NexGen Yachting sells fractional co-ownership shares in luxury sailing catamarans (typically a 1/6 share for up to 5 weeks a year, up to 1/4 for premium vessels), with a fleet of 8+ boats that rotates between the Mediterranean in summer and the Caribbean in winter. Crew, management, maintenance, and charter brokerage are bundled, and a separate charter business lets prospects experience the fleet before buying. It serves affluent US-based buyers (roughly 40-65, $500K+ net worth) who want the private-yacht lifestyle without the full cost or operational burden of sole ownership.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Get a newly signed co-owner from contract to their first booked ownership week - the visceral 'aha' of stepping aboard - as fast as possible, while reinforcing a high-ticket buying decision.
campaigns.trigger: Co-ownership contract signed / new owner account created. Send within minutes of signing.
campaigns.segment: Newly signed co-owners; branch by vessel and first season (Mediterranean summer vs Caribbean winter).
campaigns.rationale: A $250K-$4M+ decision carries real buyer's-remorse risk; an instant, white-glove welcome that turns the abstract purchase into a concrete first sailing week locks in confidence and drives the only activation that matters here.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome, set expectations for the white-glove handoff, and drive the single first action: schedule the first ownership week.
emails.subject: Welcome aboard, [First name] - let's choose your first week
emails.preview: Your crew, your calendar, your first sail. Here's exactly what happens next.
keyPoints: Congratulate and reaffirm the decision; name their specific vessel and first season
Introduce their dedicated onboarding coordinator with a real reply-to name
Lay out the simple path: pick your weeks, meet your crew, step aboard
Single CTA to book the first ownership week before the season fills up
Set the tone - zero logistics, fully managed; reply to this email anytime

campaigns.name: Consultation-to-Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert a post-discovery-call prospect into a signed co-owner by delivering a concrete, personalized proposal and dissolving the trust and scheduling objections.
campaigns.trigger: Discovery/sales call completed with no signed contract within ~48 hours. Proposal email sent same/next day, with a stalled-deal nudge branch.
campaigns.segment: Inbound inquiries who took a sales call; branch by vessel of interest and buyer type (lifestyle vs investment-minded).
campaigns.rationale: Ghosted post-call prospects are a top revenue leak; for this brand the proposal that shows 'this boat, these weeks, this price, this projected charter income' is the pre-purchase aha and the single biggest conversion lever.

emails.purpose: Deliver the personalized ownership proposal - the concrete aha - and make moving forward frictionless.
emails.subject: [First name], your personalized ownership proposal is inside
emails.preview: Your boat, your weeks, and projected charter income - the full picture.
keyPoints: Recap the call and reference their chosen vessel and share size
Show their specific weeks across Med summer and Caribbean winter to defuse the co-owner scheduling fear
Present charter income with hedged language ('projected', 'up to') and a clear disclaimer
Outline financing (up to 80% LTV, ~20% down, ~$50K entry) to reframe affordability
Note limited shares remaining on that vessel and offer a direct line to their advisor

campaigns.name: Lead-Magnet Nurture
campaigns.objective: Move cold brochure downloaders and inventory subscribers toward a booked consultation by proving the co-ownership model is legitimate and the economics are real.
campaigns.trigger: Ownership brochure download or inventory-alert signup; lead below the sales-ready threshold.
campaigns.segment: Brochure downloaders and newsletter subscribers; branch by stated interest (lifestyle vs charter-income / financing).
campaigns.rationale: These are the largest cold segment and they go dark after the first touch; with trust the category's #1 objection, a proof-led email with a real owner's numbers is what earns the sales call.

emails.purpose: Handle the #1 objection ('is this a real deal or a gimmick?') with a real owner's numbers and convert interest into a booked call.
emails.subject: The co-ownership math, with nothing left out
emails.preview: A real Lagoon 65 owner's weeks, costs, and charter income - no spin.
keyPoints: Open by naming the skepticism directly - is this real or a gimmick?
Walk through one real vessel's share price, annual costs, and projected charter offset (hedged language + disclaimer)
Address the scheduling fear: how usage weeks are allocated fairly among co-owners
Contrast owning equity against spending $20K-$80K a year chartering with no upside
Soft, low-friction CTA to a short call with an ownership advisor

campaigns.name: Win-Back / Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate leads who inquired or downloaded the brochure but never booked a call or signed, and sunset truly disengaged contacts to protect deliverability.
campaigns.trigger: 60-90 days of no opens/clicks/replies since last engagement, with no call booked.
campaigns.segment: Dormant brochure downloaders and stalled inquiries; suppress active onboarding and recent owners.
campaigns.rationale: A niche, high-intent list with no win-back leaves recoverable demand on the table; a fresh, truthful reason to return - new vessels and shares filling up - pattern-interrupts dormant dreamers without discounting.

emails.purpose: Re-open the relationship with a 'still dreaming?' hook plus a concrete, timely reason to come back.
emails.subject: Still picturing yourself in the Med, [First name]?
emails.preview: Two new catamarans just joined the fleet - and a few shares are nearly gone.
emails.cta: See what's new in the fleet
keyPoints: Warm, no-pressure open that restates the core dream they came for
Pattern-interrupt with genuinely new inventory added since they went quiet
Scarcity grounded in truth: limited shares remaining on the most popular vessels
One-click path back to the fleet or a no-obligation call
Set up the sunset - make it easy to stay subscribed or opt out

campaigns.name: Value Newsletter
campaigns.objective: Stay top-of-mind across the long sales cycle and build authority on yacht-ownership economics and destinations - without discounting.
campaigns.trigger: Scheduled cadence (every two weeks to monthly).
campaigns.segment: All engaged leads and owners; suppress active onboarding and route dormant contacts into Win-Back instead.
campaigns.rationale: With a 4-12 week (often longer) decision cycle, a value-first cadence keeps NexGen present while prospects deliberate and reinforces the financial logic that turns lifestyle dreamers into buyers.

emails.purpose: Establish authority with the signature ownership-economics story and a single soft CTA into the fleet.
emails.subject: Why owning 1/6 of a yacht beats owning the whole thing
emails.preview: The real cost of 47 idle weeks - and the math that fixes it.
keyPoints: Lead with one useful idea: the idle-weeks problem and how fractional ownership solves it
Ground it in numbers - total cost of sole ownership vs a 1/6 share (hedged claims)
Recurring habit section, e.g. 'This season's anchorage' destination spotlight
80% value / 20% soft offer with a single primary CTA
Knowledgeable-friend voice; invite replies to a real person
